mission.title: flux: Notifications - information content and filtering of info alerts
mission.description: Notifications - information content and filtering of info alerts. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
Hi, 
I am currently working on the migration of our flux(v1)/helm-operator setups to flux(v2)/Toolkit.

regarding notifications i am struggling to get a reasonable configuration regarding of information and volume.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Notifications - information content and filtering of info…
steps.description:
I don't think so,  want's to filter the info notifications not Kubernetes events.

The controller-name shows up as the username, see https://toolkit.fluxcd.io/_files/slack-info-alert.png

This is a bug tracked in https://github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/issues/236

Flux1 GC is very different to Flux2 GC. The v2 GC tracks objects without querying the Kubernetes API for each Kind, this means GC can be run under a restricted service account with no cluster wide access (unlike v1 that needs a

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Notifications - information content and filtering of info…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I don't think so,  want's to filter the info notifications not Kubernetes events.

The controller-name shows up as the username, see https://toolkit.fluxcd.io/_files/slack-info-alert.png

This is a bug tracked in https://github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/issues/236

Flux1 GC is very different to Flux2 GC.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
